01_initial_request.user_intent: To jointly predict protein intrinsic disorder and its multiple potential functions using a graph-based interaction protein language model
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Accurate prediction of intrinsic disorder and six disordered functions including protein-binding, DNA-binding, RNA-binding, ion-binding, lipid-binding, and flexible linker

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Intrinsically disordered proteins and regions (IDPs/IDRs) require functional annotations for training predictive models. [Gap] High-quality data with disordered structure annotations is needed, excluding overly long sequences. [Decision] To achieve reliable training data, chose to use DisProt database (version 9.3) to perform sequence extraction and filtering on protein entries, expecting to obtain a curated set of functionally annotated sequences.

parameters.filtering_criteria: Removed sequences whose functional regions lacked annotations for disordered structure; Excluded DP00072 sequence (>30,000 residues)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: A total of 925 sequences were obtained and used for splitting the training, validation, and independent test datasets.

references.relevance: Describes the DisProt database providing functional annotations of IDP/IDR.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequence redundancy can bias model training and evaluation. [Gap] The 925 sequences need to be clustered to ensure independence between datasets. [Decision] To achieve non-redundant dataset splits, chose to use CD-HIT algorithm to perform sequence clustering on the 925 sequences, expecting to obtain subsets with low sequence similarity.

parameters.splitting_protocol: Randomly divided clusters into five subsets (3 training, 1 validation, 1 independent test)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Sequences were clustered and divided into training (589 sequences), validation (148 sequences), and independent test DP93 (188 sequences) datasets.

references.relevance: Describes the CD-HIT algorithm used for clustering sequences.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein feature representation is essential for prediction accuracy. [Gap] Traditional features like PSSM may lack semantic context compared to language models. [Decision] To achieve superior feature representation, chose to use ProtT5 protein language model to perform embedding extraction on amino acid sequences, expecting to obtain contextual semantic encodings.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The model using ProtT5-based features consistently achieved the highest performance in predicting disorder and disordered functions compared to PSSM and One-hot.

references.relevance: Describes the ProtT5 protein language model employed for embeddings.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multiple disordered functions are dependent and interrelated. [Gap] Current methods do not adequately take into account functional correlations. [Decision] To achieve joint prediction with enhanced correlation, chose to use DisoFLAG (GiPLM architecture) to perform model construction integrating Bi-GRU, Attention-GRU, and Graph-based interaction units, expecting to obtain a model capable of learning semantic correlation features.

parameters.architecture_components: Bi-GRU layer, Attention-based GRU layer, 6 fully connected layers for function embedding, Graph-based interaction unit (GiU), GCN layer
parameters.graph_initialization: Information Gain (IG) matrix pre-calculated on training dataset

02_agent_trajectory.observation: The GiPLM architecture was established to model six disordered functions and their correlations as a functional graph.

references.relevance: Describes the Bi-GRU, Attention mechanism, and GCN layer components.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The model needs to be optimized to minimize prediction error. [Gap] Model variables and hyper-parameters require tuning. [Decision] To achieve optimal predictive performance, chose to use Binary cross-entropy loss function to perform model training and optimization on the validation dataset, expecting to obtain trained model weights.

parameters.optimization_target: Minimum loss function values on the validation dataset

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All model variables and hyper-parameters were optimized according to the minimum loss function values.

references.relevance: Describes the loss function combination used for training.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Predictive performance needs to be validated on independent data. [Gap] Performance on unseen data (DP93) is unknown. [Decision] To achieve performance benchmarking, chose to use DisoFLAG to perform evaluation on the DP93 independent test dataset, expecting to obtain metrics like AUC, MCC, and BACC.

parameters.metrics: AUC, AUPR, APS, F_max, MCC, BACC

02_agent_trajectory.observation: DisoFLAG achieved better performance in predicting disordered protein-binding, DNA-binding, RNA-binding, lipid-binding, and linkers than current tools.

references.relevance: Describes the threshold-independent and binary evaluation metrics used.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model stability needs verification on newer data versions. [Gap] Performance on latest DisProt version (9.4) is unknown. [Decision] To achieve stability verification, chose to use DisoFLAG to perform evaluation on the DP94 independent test dataset, expecting to obtain AUC metrics for comparison.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Performance was significantly better in predicting disordered protein-binding, DNA-binding, and linkers; decreased in RNA-binding and lipid-bindings.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Community standards (CAID2) provide rigorous benchmarks. [Gap] Performance relative to CAID2 top methods is unknown. [Decision] To achieve community benchmark validation, chose to use DisoFLAG to perform evaluation on CAID2 disorder-binding and disorder-linker datasets, expecting to obtain AUC and APS scores.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DisoFLAG's protein-binding predictor generated highest quality predictions (AUC=0.879, APS=0.563); Linker predictor achieved AUC=0.8.

references.relevance: Describes the CAID2 challenge and datasets.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Intrinsic disorder prediction is a core task. [Gap] Performance on standard disorder datasets (NOX/PDB) needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve disorder prediction validation, chose to use DisoFLAG to perform evaluation on CAID2 Disorder-NOX and Disorder-PDB datasets, expecting to obtain AUC and APS rankings.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DisoFLAG achieved second rank with AUC of 0.836 on Disorder-NOX; comparable performance on Disorder-PDB.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional correlations contribute to prediction but need interpretability. [Gap] The specific contribution of correlations is not quantified. [Decision] To achieve interpretability analysis, chose to use Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) to perform contribution scoring on the graph-based interaction units, expecting to obtain importance scores of functional correlations.

parameters.calculation: Sum relevance scores of all true-positive propensity predictions on DP93

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Functional correlations consistently made a positive contribution to the prediction of six disordered functions.

references.relevance: Describes the LRP method used for investigation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Real-world applicability needs demonstration. [Gap] Performance on specific biologically relevant proteins is unknown. [Decision] To achieve case study validation, chose to use DisoFLAG to perform prediction on HIV-1 Vif protein (DP00875), expecting to obtain propensity scores and binary results for binding regions.

parameters.threshold: Threshold that achieves the maximum F1 score

02_agent_trajectory.observation: DisoFLAG was the only method to simultaneously identify complete disordered protein-binding and lipid-binding regions with lowest false-positives.

references.relevance: Describes the HIV-1 Vif protein and its functional context.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: CAID2 evaluation protocols and Independent test datasets (DP93, DP94)

AUC_protein_binding.interpretation: Highest quality predictions on disorder-binding dataset.

APS_protein_binding.interpretation: Average precision score on disorder-binding dataset.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. DisoFLAG offers accurate and comprehensive predictions of disordered functions, extending the current coverage of computationally predicted disordered function categories with superior performance on CAID2 benchmarks.
